race.name: Sea Otter Ciclobrava
race.tagline: 140km Costa Brava bliss: Girona's ultimate non-competitive road romp amid Sea Otter mania.

vitals.field_size: Over 1,000 cyclists flooding Girona's paved paradise, part of Sea Otter's 6,000+ participant frenzy.
vitals.registration: Limited spots open early spring; includes event T-shirt, timing chip, drink/nutrition stations, medical aid, plus full Sea Otter expo access and bike demos.
vitals.prize_purse: Non-competitive fun ride: no purse, all glory.
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple drink and nutrition stations along routes, with medical support.
vitals.cutoff_time: Flexible for non-competitive format; ample time to enjoy festival vibes.
distance_options: 70km: 43mi, 2,625ft — accessible intro with Santa Pellaia, Madremanya, Montjuïc climbs.
100km: 62mi, 3,776ft — rolling challenge tackling Romanyà, La Ganga, Montjuïc, Madremanya.
140km: 87mi, 6,562ft — epic flagship assaulting Sant Grau d’Ardenya, Romanyà, La Ganga, Montjuïc.

climate.description: September in Girona delivers perfect cycling weather: temps in the 70s°F, minimal rain, and sunny skies that make every pedal stroke feel like vacation. Low humidity keeps efforts crisp, while coastal breezes add a refreshing edge. Ideal for crushing climbs without summer scorch or autumn chill.

terrain.primary: Paved road with Girona's iconic mid-mountain passes.
terrain.surface: Silky smooth paved roads, low-traffic gems where pros train—narrow, quiet, and flowing from coastal flats to punchy climbs. No gravel grit here; pure tarmac poetry through Costa Brava hamlets. Character screams 'cycling mecca' with Volta a Catalunya-flavored ports.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: Solid T2 for a non-competitive road gem embedded in Sea Otter's massive festival energy. Balances epic Girona terrain with unbeatable vibes, but lacks gravel edge and pro prestige to crack T1. Perfect for joyriders seeking pro training grounds without the sufferfest.

logistics.explanation: Girona's airport, trains, and festival parking make arrival a breeze—pure pro-pilot ease. Sea Otter's expo and demos sweeten the deal for gear junkies. Only gripe: book early or miss the party.

length.explanation: 140km hits gran fondo sweet spot—long enough to feel epic, flexible options for mortals. Crushes the 87-mile mark without epic suffering. Ideal for festival-goers balancing expo time with road conquests.

technicality.explanation: Paved roads demand zero gravel skills—just smooth descending on pro training turf. Low-traffic bliss, but no tech wizardry to thrill handlebar heroes. Roadies rejoice; gravel gods yawn.

elevation.explanation: 2,000m+ of Vuelta-grade passes like Romanyà and La Ganga deliver legit leg-burn without Tour de France insanity. Girona's mid-mountains punch above weight. Sufferfest lite, beautifully executed.

climate.explanation: September Mediterranean perfection: 70°F sun, dry roads, coastal zephyrs. Cycling nirvana where weather gods smile on every crank turn. Zero excuses, all excuses to PR.

altitude.explanation: Lowlands to 400m peaks—coastal chill, no hypoxia drama. Girona's charm thrives at sea level, not sky-high. Breathless from beauty, not thin air.

adventure.explanation: Costa Brava hamlets, sea views, hidden passes—pure exploratory joy on bike paradise turf. Non-competitive freedom amplifies the wanderlust. Festival immersion seals the thrill.

prestige.explanation: Sea Otter halo adds shine, but fun-ride status caps true glory. Girona's pro pedigree elevates it above average sportives. Solid cred for Instagram flexing.

race_quality.explanation: Organized chaos in festival frenzy: solid aid, timing, but no broom wagon battles. Non-comp vibe prioritizes fun over ferocity. Reliable, not revolutionary.

experience.explanation: Sea Otter expo, demos, global cyclists—total immersion in cycling utopia. Girona roads plus festival afterparty? Unbeatable vibe cocktail. Life highlight material.

community.explanation: Girona's year-round pro-am tribe plus 64,000 Sea Otter fanatics create instant belonging. Shared suffering (or joy) on iconic roads bonds forever. Cycling family at its finest.

field_depth.explanation: 1,000+ amateurs, few elites—party pace, not pro peloton. Lively but lacks depth for competitive souls. Perfect for social spinners, skip for KOM hunters.

value.explanation: Festival access, T-shirt, aid for modest fee—bargain bonanza. Pair with expo steals for max bang. Non-comp entry lowers barrier to bliss.

expenses.explanation: Girona's reasonable costs, easy flights via GRO—wallet-friendly Euro jaunt. Festival economies on lodging/food seal value. Skip luxury, embrace efficiency.

biased_opinion.summary: Sea Otter Ciclobrava nails the non-competitive dream: 140km of Girona's smoothest paved passes amid Costa Brava splendor, all wrapped in Sea Otter's electric expo energy. Pros train these roads for a reason—low-traffic gems like Romanyà deliver Vuelta vibes without the pain train. It's cycling vacation disguised as a sportive, perfect for joyriders craving community over contention. Weak on gravel grit and elite depth, but crushes experience and value.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Prime pick for road lovers seeking fun over fight in cycling's Spanish epicenter. Pin it if festival vibes fuel you; pass if podiums are your poison.

final_verdict.one_liner: Girona's paved party packs festival punch without the pro pain.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're a road enthusiast craving Sea Otter expo magic, Girona climbs, and zero-pressure pedaling. Skip if gravel's your gospel or you demand deep competitive fields.
final_verdict.alternatives: Unleash on UCI Gravel World Series Girona for grit; grind La Tramun Sea Otter for MTB sufferfest; escape to Mediterranean Epic Gran Fondo for longer hauls.

logistics.airport: Girona-Costa Brava (GRO) right on doorstep—quickest hop; Barcelona (BCN) 1.5hrs by train/road for more flights. Festival shuttles and parking streamline arrivals.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Base in Girona expo zone for walkable festival access; book Airbnbs early amid Sea Otter surge. Shoulder-season rates still reasonable.
logistics.food: Girona's tapas paradise fuels pre/post-ride feasts; festival food trucks handle expo hunger. Coastal seafood and pro cafes abound.
logistics.packet_pickup: Seamless at Sea Otter expo—grab kit, T-shirt, chip amid demo bikes.
logistics.parking: Dedicated festival lots at Passeig de la Devesa start; ample for cyclists.

history.founder: Club Esportiu Bike Catalunya, Sea Otter Europe organizers
history.origin_story: Born in 2018 as Sea Otter Europe's chill Sunday counterpart to gravel grinders, Ciclobrava lets amateurs soak Girona's pro roads sans stopwatch stress. Organized by local heroes Club Esportiu Bike Catalunya, it exploded within the festival's massive growth. Now a staple delving Costa Brava passes, cementing Girona as cycling's global playground.
notable_moments: Integrated into Sea Otter's 64,000-visitor juggernaut by edition 7.
Evolved to 70/100/140km options suiting all abilities.
Drew 1,000+ registrants chasing Costa Brava dreams.
Hit eighth festival edition by 2026, boosting Girona's hub status.
Limited reg opens April, selling out fast.
history.reputation: Beloved as Girona's fun-road gateway, blending accessibility with iconic terrain. Sea Otter tie-in amps prestige without competitive pressure. Riders rave about vibe over verdict.

course_description.character: Paved poetry through Girona's Pyrenees-Mediterranean embrace: low-traffic roads wind from urban start to coastal climbs and charming villages. Non-competitive freedom lets you savor Vuelta passes like Romanyà and La Ganga at your pace. Festival finish at Devesa promenade caps the joyride amid cheering crowds.

suffering_zones.label: Sant Grau d’Ardenya
suffering_zones.desc: First big test assaults this coastal beast early, rewarding with panoramic payoffs. Steady gradients build rhythm before real pain arrives. Iconic for long-route warriors.

suffering_zones.label: Romanyà Vuelta Wall
suffering_zones.desc: Vuelta 2019 stage 2 legend demands respect with punchy ramps amid Ardenya forests. Pro-proven pitch separates weekenders from roadies. Descend into glory.

suffering_zones.label: La Ganga Grinder
suffering_zones.desc: Vuelta stage 3 crusher grinds legs with relentless gradient in remote beauty. Hidden gem pass tests resolve before coastal relief. Signature suffer zone.

suffering_zones.label: Montjuïc Finale
suffering_zones.desc: City-approaching kicker flips fatigue to triumph as Girona nears. Shared by all routes, it's the crowd-pleasing capstone. Expo awaits.

course_description.signature_challenge: The quadruple-pass onslaught of Sant Grau d’Ardenya, Romanyà, La Ganga, and Montjuïc on the 140km—Vuelta-flavored beasts strung like pearls through Costa Brava, blending climb-drama with sea-view salvation for unforgettable road romance.

guide_variables.race_terrain: Paved roads with mountain passes and coastal views
guide_variables.race_weather: Mild Mediterranean September: 70°F, sunny, low rain

non_negotiables.requirement: Base build to 100km+ weekly with 1500m+ elevation
non_negotiables.why: Girona's passes like Romanyà demand sustained power over 5+ hours. Non-comp doesn't mean easy—2,000m cumulative crushes unfit legs. Nail volume early to savor festival fun.

non_negotiables.requirement: Practice low-traffic descents and group riding
non_negotiables.why: Pro roads mean fast packs and tech drops; safety first amid 1,000 riders. Builds confidence for Vuelta walls. Essential for stress-free bliss.
